ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Begedil; closest match is potato cake (FOODON_03315671). Begedil is a deep-fried mashed potato patty with egg coating, shallots, and optional minced meat, which is more specific than the generic potato cake concept.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Potato products and preparations) is the closest defensible category. No specific code exists for Begedil as a named Southeast Asian dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Begedil or equivalent Southeast Asian potato patty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Begedil is one piece weighing approximately 50 grams, commonly served as a side dish alongside noodle soups such as Mee Soto or rice dishes such as Nasi Padang.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are sourced from the HPB Singapore food composition database for Southeast Asian potato-based dishes. Begedil is a recognized dish in the Singapore food context. Values for micronutrients not directly listed in HPB have been cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) for mashed potato and egg components and are noted as estimated where applicable.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Begedil as a named dish. GI of 75 is estimated based on the closest analogous food: mashed potato (GI ~83 per University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by the presence of egg coating, frying fat, and protein content which partially slow gastric emptying. A conservative estimate of GI 75 (high category) is applied. Glycemic load per 50g serving = (75 × 19.8 × 0.5) / 100 = 7.4g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (320 mg per 100g) from seasoning and deep-frying process
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Begedil intake to one piece per meal and avoid pairing with other high-sodium dishes such as soy-sauce-based gravies; consider air-frying and reducing added salt to lower sodium by approximately 30–40%.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~75) driven by mashed potato base with rapid starch digestion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict Begedil to one small piece (50g) per meal, pair with non-starchy vegetables and lean protein to blunt postprandial glucose response, and monitor blood glucose after consumption given the high-GI potato base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (12.5g/100g) from deep-frying, with saturated fat (~2.1g) and estimated cholesterol (~85mg) from egg coating
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Begedil to occasional consumption, preferring baked or air-fried preparation to reduce total fat by up to 50%, and should account for its sodium and fat contribution within daily cardiovascular dietary targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade vegetable (potato) and pro-inflammatory frying oils may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade vegetables should consume Begedil cautiously and consider substituting with non-nightshade starchy vegetables; frying in anti-inflammatory oils such as olive oil may partially mitigate inflammatory risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Begedil is a traditional Malay and Indonesian deep-fried potato patty widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. Its name derives from the Dutch word 'perkadell', reflecting the colonial culinary heritage of the region. The dish is prepared by deep-frying or boiling potatoes, mashing them, and combining the mash with fried shallots, Chinese celery (daun sop), spring onions, white pepper, and nutmeg. Many versions incorporate a small quantity of minced beef or chicken for additional protein and savory depth. The mixture is shaped into small round discs approximately 5–6 cm in diameter, dipped in beaten egg, and deep-fried in hot oil until the exterior is golden brown with a characteristic thin, lacy, crispy egg crust, while the interior remains soft, fluffy, and savory. Begedil is commonly served as a side dish accompanying Nasi Soto, Mee Soto, or Nasi Padang, and is also eaten as a standalone savory snack. Nutritionally, each 50g piece provides approximately 107 kcal, 2.9g protein, 6.3g fat, and 9.9g carbohydrate. The high glycemic index (~75) due to the mashed potato base makes portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The sodium content of 320mg per 100g warrants attention for those with hypertension. Begedil is generally halal and gluten-free but contains egg as an allergen. It is a culturally significant comfort food in the Malay-Indonesian culinary tradition.
common_user_queries: Is begedil the same as perkedel?
What is the GI of begedil?
What dishes is begedil served with?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from the HPB Singapore food composition database for Southeast Asian potato-based dishes, which is an approved primary regional source. Begedil is a recognized dish in the Singapore and Malaysian food context. Micronutrient values for fatty acid fractions (saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated) and cholesterol are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for mashed potato and egg components, as HPB does not publish full fatty acid profiles for this dish. GI is an analogous estimate based on mashed potato GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.1g), monounsaturated fat (5.2g), polyunsaturated fat (3.8g), trans fat (0.1g), and cholesterol (85mg) per 100g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for mashed potato (USDA FDC ID 170093) and whole egg (USDA FDC ID 748967) components, as HPB Singapore does not publish full fatty acid profiles for Begedil. These values should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis if available. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for Begedil as a deep-fried preparation is not reported in the HPB Singapore database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific dish combination; limited published data for this traditional preparation. 3. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on mashed potato (GI ~83, University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by egg, fat, and protein content. No direct GI measurement for Begedil was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2024. 4. The dietary_tags in the input listed 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based'; however, many standard Begedil recipes include minced beef or chicken, and the egg coating is non-vegan. Dietary flags have been set to is_vegetarian: false and is_vegan: false to reflect the most common preparation. Consumers should verify with the specific vendor. 5. Tamil and Hindi local names are not applicable as Begedil is a Malay/Indonesian dish without established Tamil or Hindi naming conventions; these fields are set to null. 6. FoodOn match FOODON_03315671 (potato cake) and FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Potato products and preparations) are approximate matches; no exact ontology code exists for Begedil as a named Southeast Asian dish.
